properties.name: Body
properties.constraint: 300 (As part of a highlighted section, the content should be limited to a single sentence.)

properties.name: Padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the pattern
properties.constraint: he CTA section uses deep padding on both the top and bottom.

properties.name: Section rule
properties.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new pattern
properties.constraint: Only use the default style for the section-level rule

properties.name: Link CTA
properties.description: The text link used for contextual actions or pathways to more information. It uses a chevron (›) to indicate navigation to additional content or pages.

table.name: Body
table.description:
Optional supplementary body text used to explain the reason for the action.
This element is available only in the block variant and appears directly beneath the heading.

table.name: CTA
table.description:
The interactive element(s) that trigger the intended action. Depending on the variant, this may appear as:
One or more inline text links (link-style CTA), or
A dedicated CTA block containing buttons or structured actions
This element is required and represents the functional core of the CTA section.

table.name: Heading
table.description: A concise, action-oriented heading that communicates the purpose of the call to action.

table.name: Rule
table.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new section.

properties.name: Primary CTA
properties.description: The CTA used with the positive button style. It indicates a high-priority, positive action and is reserved for hero areas or other key call-to-action sections.

properties.name: Secondary CTA
properties.description: The CTA using the default button style. It is used for most actions that are positive but not primary, and may serve as the main CTA in non-hero sections.

properties.name: Link CTA
properties.description: The text link used for contextual actions or pathways to more information. It uses a chevron (›) to indicate navigation to additional content or pages.
